extraction.one_line_summary: CAVENDISH Mark won the Rome processional stage from distance after COENS successfully disrupted GHIGGI's intermediate sprint hunt in closing kilometers
tactical_patterns: COENS actively blocked intermediate sprint points to deny GHIGGI the classification, revealing sprinters' willingness to contest secondary competitions on final stages.
CAVENDISH launched from distance off Colombian GAVILIA's wheel rather than waiting for late-stage positioning, suggesting confidence in sustained power over positioning.
THOMAS Geraint contributed to leadout work despite no GC incentive, indicating Sky/INEOS cultural patterns persist even on ceremonial stages.

rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: first win in four years for team shows sustained sprint power when launching early, not reliant on perfect positioning
rider_signals.COENS Lars: positive: tactical awareness to disrupt GHIGGI's intermediate sprint bid demonstrates non-GC riders actively racing secondary classifications on final stages
rider_signals.GAVILIA Juan Sebastian: neutral: Colombian provided leadout that CAVENDISH used as launch platform, suggesting domestique role shift from climbing support
